speaker: ANDROMACHE
text: When was my lord so much ungently tempered

speaker: ANDROMACHE
text: To stop his ears against admonishment?

speaker: ANDROMACHE
text: Unarm, unarm, and do not fight today.

speaker: HECTOR
text: You train me to offend you. Get you in.

speaker: HECTOR
text: By all the everlasting gods, I’ll go!

speaker: ANDROMACHE
text: My dreams will sure prove ominous to the day.

speaker: HECTOR
text: No more, I say.

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: Where is my brother Hector?

speaker: ANDROMACHE
text: Here, sister, armed and bloody in intent.

speaker: ANDROMACHE
text: Consort with me in loud and dear petition;

speaker: ANDROMACHE
text: Pursue we him on knees. For I have dreamt

speaker: ANDROMACHE
text: Of bloody turbulence, and this whole night

speaker: ANDROMACHE
text: Hath nothing been but shapes and forms of slaughter.

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: O, ’tis true!

speaker: HECTOR
text: Ho! Bid my trumpet sound!

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: No notes of sally, for the heavens, sweet brother!

speaker: HECTOR
text: Begone, I say. The gods have heard me swear.

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: The gods are deaf to hot and peevish vows.

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: They are polluted off’rings more abhorred

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: Than spotted livers in the sacrifice.

speaker: ANDROMACHE
text: O, be persuaded! Do not count it holy

speaker: ANDROMACHE
text: To hurt by being just. It is as lawful,

speaker: ANDROMACHE
text: For we would give much, to use violent thefts

speaker: ANDROMACHE
text: And rob in the behalf of charity.

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: It is the purpose that makes strong the vow,

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: But vows to every purpose must not hold.

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: Unarm, sweet Hector.

speaker: HECTOR
text: Hold you still, I say.

speaker: HECTOR
text: Mine honor keeps the weather of my fate.

speaker: HECTOR
text: Life every man holds dear, but the dear man

speaker: HECTOR
text: Holds honor far more precious-dear than life.

speaker: HECTOR
text: How now, young man? Meanest thou to fight today?

speaker: ANDROMACHE
text: Cassandra, call my father to persuade.

speaker: HECTOR
text: No, faith, young Troilus, doff thy harness, youth.

speaker: HECTOR
text: I am today i’ th’ vein of chivalry.

speaker: HECTOR
text: Let grow thy sinews till their knots be strong,

speaker: HECTOR
text: And tempt not yet the brushes of the war.

speaker: HECTOR
text: Unarm thee, go, and doubt thou not, brave boy,

speaker: HECTOR
text: I’ll stand today for thee and me and Troy.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Brother, you have a vice of mercy in you

speaker: TROILUS
text: Which better fits a lion than a man.

speaker: HECTOR
text: What vice is that? Good Troilus, chide me for it.

speaker: TROILUS
text: When many times the captive Grecian falls,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Even in the fan and wind of your fair sword,

speaker: TROILUS
text: You bid them rise and live.

speaker: HECTOR
text: O, ’tis fair play.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Fool’s play, by heaven. Hector.

speaker: HECTOR
text: How now? How now?

speaker: TROILUS
text: For th’ love of all the gods,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Let’s leave the hermit Pity with our mother,

speaker: TROILUS
text: And when we have our armors buckled on,

speaker: TROILUS
text: The venomed Vengeance ride upon our swords,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Spur them to ruthful work, rein them from ruth.

speaker: HECTOR
text: Fie, savage, fie!

speaker: TROILUS
text: Hector, then ’tis wars.

speaker: HECTOR
text: Troilus, I would not have you fight today.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Who should withhold me?

speaker: TROILUS
text: Not fate, obedience, nor the hand of Mars,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Beck’ning with fiery truncheon my retire;

speaker: TROILUS
text: Not Priamus and Hecuba on knees,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Their eyes o’er-gallèd with recourse of tears;

speaker: TROILUS
text: Nor you, my brother, with your true sword drawn

speaker: TROILUS
text: Opposed to hinder me, should stop my way,

speaker: TROILUS
text: But by my ruin.

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: Lay hold upon him, Priam; hold him fast.

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: He is thy crutch. Now if thou loose thy stay,

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: Thou on him leaning, and all Troy on thee,

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: Fall all together.

speaker: PRIAM
text: Come, Hector, come. Go back.

speaker: PRIAM
text: Thy wife hath dreamt, thy mother hath had visions,

speaker: PRIAM
text: Cassandra doth foresee, and I myself

speaker: PRIAM
text: Am like a prophet suddenly enrapt

speaker: PRIAM
text: To tell thee that this day is ominous.

speaker: PRIAM
text: Therefore, come back.

speaker: HECTOR
text: Aeneas is afield,

speaker: HECTOR
text: And I do stand engaged to many Greeks,

speaker: HECTOR
text: Even in the faith of valor, to appear

speaker: HECTOR
text: This morning to them.

speaker: PRIAM
text: Ay, but thou shalt not go.

speaker: HECTOR
text: I must not break my faith.

speaker: HECTOR
text: You know me dutiful; therefore, dear sir,

speaker: HECTOR
text: Let me not shame respect, but give me leave

speaker: HECTOR
text: To take that course by your consent and voice

speaker: HECTOR
text: Which you do here forbid me, royal Priam.

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: O Priam, yield not to him!

speaker: ANDROMACHE
text: Do not, dear father.

speaker: HECTOR
text: Andromache, I am offended with you.

speaker: HECTOR
text: Upon the love you bear me, get you in.

speaker: TROILUS
text: This foolish, dreaming, superstitious girl

speaker: TROILUS
text: Makes all these bodements.

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: O farewell, dear Hector.

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: Look how thou diest! Look how thy eye turns pale!

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: Look how thy wounds do bleed at many vents!

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: Hark, how Troy roars, how Hecuba cries out,

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: How poor Andromache shrills her dolor forth!

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: Behold, distraction, frenzy, and amazement,

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: Like witless antics, one another meet,

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: And all cry “Hector! Hector’s dead! O, Hector!”

speaker: TROILUS
text: Away, away!

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: Farewell.—Yet soft! Hector, I take my leave.

speaker: CASSANDRA
text: Thou dost thyself and all our Troy deceive.

speaker: HECTOR
text: You are amazed, my liege, at her exclaim.

speaker: HECTOR
text: Go in and cheer the town. We’ll forth and fight,

speaker: HECTOR
text: Do deeds worth praise, and tell you them at night.

speaker: PRIAM
text: Farewell. The gods with safety stand about thee!

speaker: TROILUS
text: They are at it, hark! Proud Diomed, believe,

speaker: TROILUS
text: I come to lose my arm or win my sleeve.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Do you hear, my lord? Do you hear?

speaker: TROILUS
text: What now?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Here’s a letter come from yond poor girl.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Let me read.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: A whoreson phthisic, a whoreson rascally

speaker: PANDARUS
text: phthisic so troubles me, and the foolish fortune of

speaker: PANDARUS
text: this girl, and what one thing, what another, that I

speaker: PANDARUS
text: shall leave you one o’ these days. And I have a

speaker: PANDARUS
text: rheum in mine eyes too, and such an ache in my

speaker: PANDARUS
text: bones that, unless a man were cursed, I cannot tell

speaker: PANDARUS
text: what to think on ’t.—What says she there?

speaker: TROILUS
text: Words, words, mere words, no matter from the heart.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Th’ effect doth operate another way.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Go, wind, to wind! There turn and change together.

speaker: TROILUS
text: My love with words and errors still she feeds,

speaker: TROILUS
text: But edifies another with her deeds.
